step_by_step_thinking:
The staining technique used here, triphenyltetrazolium chloride (TTC), stains viable myocardial cells red/brown because they reduce TTC to formazan crystals, whereas dead myocardial cells do not stain since they cannot perform this reduction reaction. Since the patient had a myocardial infarction, there would be areas of dead myocardium that did not undergo proper cellular respiration and thus would appear unstained upon application of TTC.
